sanskrit:
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम्।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन॥
transliteration:
kathaṃ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitum |
kula-kṣaya-kṛtaṃ doṣaṃ prapaśyadbhir janārdana ||
literal_meaning: How should we not know / to turn away from sin / we who clearly see / the crime of destroying the family, O Janārdana?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s conscience surfaces as moral horror: “Even if they are blinded by greed, we who see the catastrophe must refuse to cooperate.”
psychological_state: Heightened moral sensitivity that splits the world into ‘the seeing self’ and ‘the blind other’, producing paralysis rather than action.
trigger_scenario: A team-member realizes the company’s product harms users; colleagues justify it with profit. The whistle-blower freezes, asking, ‘If they can’t see the damage, must I stay silent or quit?’
anchor_text: Refuse to cooperate with wrong, even when others rush in.
